title: Glyph Foundry
description: Hand-draw your own bitmap font on a pixel grid and watch it render live as you type, then export it as a real font asset.
concept: Glyph Foundry is a pixel-grid bitmap font editor: pick a character from the A-Z/0-9/punctuation palette, draw pixels on a 10x14 grid with mouse or touch, and see your custom typeface rendered live in a large preview as you type any string. A one-click generator seeds every glyph from a system font so you start from a real alphabet instead of a blank grid, and per-glyph tools (clear, invert, mirror, reset) make quick edits painless.
features: 10x14 pixel grid glyph editor with pointer-based click-and-drag drawing (mouse + touch)
Character palette covering A-Z, 0-9, space, and punctuation (. , ! ? ' -) with a defined-glyph indicator dot
Live large-scale text preview with adjustable scale and letter-spacing sliders plus color swatches/custom color picker
Generate starter font: procedurally seeds all glyphs from a system font via offscreen canvas supersampling + alpha thresholding
Per-glyph tools: reset from system font, clear, invert, mirror horizontal, and single-level undo
Font library: save/load/duplicate/rename/delete named font projects in localStorage, with autosave of in-progress work
Export PNG spritesheet (labeled glyph grid) via canvas.toBlob and real file download
Export/import JSON font format ({name, gridW, gridH, glyphs}) for full round-trip re-loading
Keyboard shortcuts: arrow keys to move between glyphs, c to clear, i to invert, u to undo
1px/2px brush size toggle for faster coarse drawing

interaction.mouse: Click and drag on the pixel grid to draw/erase; drag sliders to adjust preview scale and spacing
interaction.click: Click a character in the palette to edit it; click tool buttons to clear/invert/mirror/generate/export/save
interaction.touch: Tap and drag on the pixel grid to draw (Pointer Events with touch-action: none prevents page scroll while drawing)
interaction.keyboard: Arrow keys move between glyphs in the palette; c clears, i inverts, u undoes the active glyph
